yafu_siqs_baseline.scaling_argument: SIQS is L[1/2, 1+o(1)] = exp((1+o(1)) * sqrt(ln N * ln ln N)). For each +10 digits, time multiplies by ~8-10x. This is the best known single-core performance for numbers under 100 digits. Any novel approach should aim for a slower growth rate — e.g. L[1/3] grows ~3-4x per +10 digits, L[1/4] would grow ~2x.

mpqs.scaling_argument: MPQS implementation (library/mpqs.c). L[1/2] with single-prime A values, 2LP variation.

sss.scaling_argument: Smooth Subsum Search (Hittmeir 2023). L[1/2] but with CRT-based structured candidate generation instead of sieving. Constructs x values where pol(x) is guaranteed divisible by several FB primes, then tests smaller cofactor.

cfrac.scaling_argument: Continued Fraction Algorithm. L[1/2] like QS but with single expansion (no multiple polynomials). Naturally bounded values but limited relation generation rate.
